facts_disclosed: EV charger side gig dead — needs journeyman license, insurance too expensive

turns.content: man im kinda annoyed rn can I just vent for a sec
Yeah of course, go for it. What's going on?

so I had this plan to start doin EV charger installs on the side right like at peoples houses on weekends. my buddy was makin crazy money doin it and I figured why not I already know how to run 240 circuits
That sounds like a solid idea honestly -- EV charger installs are in high demand right now and if you've already got the electrical skills, you're ahead of most people. What happened?
well I finally brought it up to my dad this weekend and he basically shut it down. like not in a mean way but he was real straight with me about it
Ah, that's tough. What was his reasoning? Sometimes dads see stuff we miss, especially if he's been in the industry a while.

he said first of all I cant be pullin permits on my own without a journeyman license which I mean I knew that part but I figured id work under his license or whatever but he was like nah thats not how it works if youre doin side jobs on your own thats a whole different thing

He's right about that. In Massachusetts, pulling permits independently requires your own journeyman license -- you can't really use someone else's for side work that isn't going through their company. Did he say anything about running it through his business instead?

yeah I asked about that too and he said he doesnt wanna take on the liability for weekend gigs that he cant supervise. plus he looked into it and the liability insurance for doin that kind of work independently is like 3 grand a year bro THREE THOUSAND just for insurance

Yeah, $3,000/year for liability insurance as a solo electrician doing install work is actually pretty standard unfortunately. And it makes sense that your dad doesn't want the risk on his company's insurance for jobs he's not overseeing -- one bad install and it could affect his whole business.

exactly and I get it like I do. its just frustrating cuz I was wicked hyped about it you know I thought I had this whole plan figured out and now its just dead. like forget about the EV charger thing bro its not happening

I totally get the frustration. You had a real plan, the demand is there, and it felt like a no-brainer. But it sounds like the timing just isn't right -- not the idea itself. Are you close to getting your journeyman license?

I mean I passed the exam back in march but theres like a whole process with the hours and the paperwork and honestly even if I had the license tomorrow I still cant afford 3 grand for insurance right now. so yeah its off the table for real

That's good that you passed the exam at least -- that's a major piece of it. The insurance cost is the real blocker right now, but that's the kind of thing that could be doable down the line when you've saved up a bit more. It's not a permanent no, it's just a not-right-now.

yeah I guess thats one way to look at it. my dad was cool about it too he wasnt tryna crush my dreams or nothin he just said kid youre not ready yet and when you are well do it the right way. which I mean hes right but it still sucks

That's actually really solid advice from him. "Do it the right way" is the kind of thing you'll appreciate later even if it stings now. He clearly isn't against the idea -- he just wants you set up properly before you take that leap. In the meantime, you're still building experience and hours every day at work, which only makes you more ready when the time comes.
true true. alright im done bein salty about it lol thanks for lettin me vent bro I just needed to get that out

Anytime man, that's what I'm here for. It's totally fair to be frustrated -- you had a good plan and it didn't work out the way you wanted. But you've got the skills and the exam under your belt, so when the timing lines up it'll happen. Hang in there.
